name: Fool
effect: After making a d20 roll, and before the DM describes the outcome, you can roll another d20. You must use the second d20 roll as the final roll.

name: Magician
effect: You cast a spell that you have prepared without expending a spell slot.

name: Priestess
effect: You gain advantage on all Wisdom based Skill Checks and Saving Throws for one hour.

name: Empress
effect: You cast a 1st-level 'Detect Evil and Good' spell once.

name: Emperor
effect: You cast a 1st-level 'Shield' spell once.

name: Priest/Hierophant
effect: You gain a +5 to all Wisdom based Skill Checks and Saving Throws for one hour.

name: Lovers
effect: You and a target you can see within 300ft of you recover 1d20 Hit Points.

name: Chariot
effect: You can immediately take the Dash action once.

name: Strength
effect: You can immediately make two unarmed strikes or melee weapon attacks.

name: Hermit
effect: You gain advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks for one hour.

name: Wheel of Fortune
effect: Before making a d20 roll you can choose to bend luck. Roll 3d20s. If the majority of the rolls are even numbers you can use the highest roll as your final roll. If the majority of the rolls are odd numbers you must use the lowest number as your final roll.

name: Justice
effect: After a creature damages you with an unarmed strike or melee weapon attack and you are left with at least 1 Hit Point, the attacker is immediately damaged for 1d20 force damage.

name: Hanged Man
effect: You donate your lifeforce to a creature within 10ft of you. Roll a d4, d6, d8, d10, d12, or d20. The result is the amount of necrotic damage you receive and the amount of Hit Points the target creature recovers.

name: Death
effect: You gain +5 to Death Saving Throws.

name: Temperance
effect: You and 1d6 creatures you can see within 300ft of you gain 1d20 Temporary Hit Points for one hour.

name: Devil
effect: You wrench the lifeforce from a creature within 10ft of you. Roll a d4, d6, d8, d10, d12, or d20. The result is the amount of fire damage you inflict on the target and the amount of Hit Points you recover.

name: Tower
effect: A creature you can see within 10ft of you immediately gains the restrained condition. A creature restrained this way can use its action to make a Strength check against a DC 20. On a success, it frees itself.

name: Star
effect: You cast a 4th-level 'Scry' spell once.

name: Moon
effect: You cast a 5th-level 'Major Illusion' spell once.

name: Sun
effect: You cast a 6th-level 'Sunbeam' spell once.

name: Judgement
effect: You cast a 2nd-level 'Zone of Truth' spell once.

name: The World
effect: You assemble your allies at your side. Choose 1d6 targets you can see within 300ft of you and teleport them to an unoccupied space within 10ft of you.
